AISuggestion.name: IncompleteDesignVariant_Test5
AISuggestion.description: The specification requires a barometer sensor whose measurements are acquired by the controller and transmitted as barometric pressure, but the model has no Barometer block, no Barometer part instance, and no connection producing Controller.BarometerData.
AISuggestion.howToSolve: Add a Barometer block with a state machine that acquires and sends pressure data, instantiate it in the system structure, connect its output to Controller.BarometerData, and extend the controller behavior to receive that input before transmission.

AISuggestion.name: IncompleteDesignVariant_Test4
AISuggestion.description: The model is missing the barometer sensing chain required by the specification. Although pressure is listed in transmitted data and a controller input type for barometer data exists, there is no Barometer block, no instantiated barometer part, no connection to the Controller, and no controller reception step that updates pressure from barometer measurements.
AISuggestion.howToSolve: Add a Barometer sensor block and corresponding signal type, instantiate the barometer in the system structure, connect it to the Controller, and extend the Controller state machine with a reception step for barometer data before derived-data computation and VHF transmission.

AISuggestion.name: IncompleteDesignVariant_Test3
AISuggestion.description: The specification requires a barometer sensor and transmission of barometric pressure, but the model has no instantiated Barometer block, no connection feeding Controller.BarometerData, and the controller behavior never acquires pressure before sending data to the VHF transmitter.
AISuggestion.howToSolve: Add a Barometer part with an output item carrying pressure, connect it to Controller.BarometerData, and extend the Controller state machine with a pressure-acquisition step that stores the received value before computing derived measurements and transmitting the measurement set.

AISuggestion.name: IncompleteDesignVariant_Test2
AISuggestion.description: The specification requires the buoy to be equipped with a barometer and to transmit barometric pressure via the VHF transmitter, but the model has no Barometer part, no connection feeding the controller from such a sensor, and no controller behavior that acquires BarometerData before transmission.
AISuggestion.howToSolve: Add a dedicated Barometer block and instance, define and connect its BarometerData flow to the Controller, and extend the Controller state machine so it explicitly receives BarometerData and updates pressure before sending the transmission payload.

AISuggestion.name: IncompleteDesignVariant_Test1
AISuggestion.description: The model is missing the barometer sensor required by the specification. Although the Controller declares an input for BarometerData and transmits pressure, there is no Barometer block, no BarometerData producer, and no connection to the Controller, so barometric pressure cannot be acquired as specified.
AISuggestion.howToSolve: Add a Barometer block with a BarometerData output carrying pressure, instantiate it, connect it to Controller.BarometerData, and extend the Controller behavior to receive and store that pressure value before transmission.

AISuggestion.name: IncompleteDesign_Test5
AISuggestion.description: The specification requires the buoy to be equipped with a barometer and to transmit barometric pressure, but the model does not include any Barometer block, no barometer part instance, and no connection that supplies the Controller.BarometerData input. As a result, the pressure acquisition path required by the specification is incomplete.
AISuggestion.howToSolve: Add a Barometer block with an output item carrying pressure, instantiate it as a part, connect it to Controller.BarometerData, and include its acquisition behavior consistently with the other sensors so the controller can receive pressure before transmission.

AISuggestion.name: IncompleteDesign_Test4
AISuggestion.description: The specification requires a barometer sensor and transmission of barometric pressure, but the model has no Barometer block/part or connection to the Controller, and the Controller behavior never reads BarometerData before sending pressure to the VHF transmitter. As a result, pressure is modeled as transmitted without a specified acquisition source.
AISuggestion.howToSolve: Add a Barometer block and corresponding part with an output flow for BarometerData, connect it to Controller.BarometerData, and update the Controller state machine to acquire BarometerData before computing and sending measurements so that pressure originates from the required sensor.

AISuggestion.name: IncompleteDesign_Test1
AISuggestion.description: The model is missing the barometer sensor as an explicit system element, even though the specification requires the buoy to be equipped with a barometer and to provide barometric pressure to the controller for VHF transmission. The controller already expects `BarometerData` and carries a `pressure` attribute, but there is no Barometer block, no barometer item type, no behavior producing pressure data, and no connection to the controller.
AISuggestion.howToSolve: Add a `Block__Barometer` part with a `pressure` attribute, an output item carrying barometer data, a state machine that acquires and sends `pressure`, instantiate a `Barometer` part, and connect `Barometer.BarometerData` to `Controller.BarometerData`. Keep the controller responsible only for acquisition and forwarding logic, without modeling the arithmetic of derived values.

AISuggestion.name: IncompleteDesign_Test2
AISuggestion.description: The specification requires a barometer sensor and transmission of barometric pressure, but the model does not contain a Barometer block/part or a connection that provides BarometerData to the Controller. As a result, pressure cannot be acquired from the required sensor path.
AISuggestion.howToSolve: Add a dedicated Barometer block and part that emits BarometerData, connect it to Controller.BarometerData, and update the Controller behavior so it reads BarometerData before sending measurements to the VHF transmitter.

AISuggestion.name: IncompleteDesign_Test3
AISuggestion.description: The specification requires the buoy to be equipped with a barometer and to transmit barometric pressure, but the model has no Barometer block, no barometer part instance, and no connection providing Controller.BarometerData even though the controller declares that input.
AISuggestion.howToSolve: Add a Barometer block with an output signal carrying pressure, instantiate it in the system structure, connect it to Controller.BarometerData, and update the controller state machine so barometer data is actually acquired before transmission.

TURTLEGMODELING.SPECIFICATION: An oceanographic buoy is a meteorological monitoring system designed to collect and transmit data on sea state, wind conditions, and atmospheric parameters for weather monitoring and forecasting purposes. The buoy consists of sensors that measure environmental parameters, a controller that aggregates sensor data and computes derived parameters, and communication systems that enable transmission of both measured and computed data. The system to be designed is the software implementing the control logic of the buoy controller. The buoy shall transmit the following measurements via a VHF transmitter: buoy position, barometric pressure, wind direction, wind speed, wind gust, air temperature, seawater temperature, water level, relative humidity, direction of wave propagation, and wave energy spectrum. It shall be equipped with the following sensors: an IMU, a GNSS receiver, a barometer, an anemometer, an air temperature sensor, a water temperature sensor, and a humidity sensor. Wind gust shall be derived from anemometer data. Wave propagation direction and wave energy spectrum shall be derived from IMU data, and water level shall be derived from IMU and GNSS data. The model shall capture the control logic governing data acquisition, processing, and transmission, but shall not include the arithmetic computation of those derived values.

Description:The model is missing the barometer sensor as an explicit system element, even though the specification requires the

buoy to be equipped with a barometer and to provide barometric pressure to the controller for VHF transmission. The controller already expects `BarometerData` and carries a `pressure` attribute, but there is no Barometer block, no barometer item type, no behavior producing pressure data, and no connection to the controller.
How to solve:

Add a `Block__Barometer` part with a `pressure` attribute, an output item carrying barometer data, a state machine that acquires and sends `pressure`, instantiate a `Barometer` part, and connect `Barometer.BarometerData` to `Controller.BarometerData`. Keep the controller responsible only for acquisition and forwarding logic,
without modeling the arithmetic of derived values.

Our evaluation: Suggestion relevance: 9.5 Mutation script accuracy wrt to the suggestion (how to solve): 10 Model improvement: 9.5 Comments: improvement of the model wrt the identified issue. Yet it is still needed to modify a transition in the Barometer's SMD to achieve full semantic accuracy.
Mutations: add block Barometer add output signal BarometerData(int pressure) in Barometer add state acquiringMeasurements in Barometer add transition in Barometer from start to acquiringMeasurements add state afterSending__0 in Barometer add transition in Barometer from acquiringMeasurements to afterSending__0 with "BarometerData(pressure)" add transition in Barometer from afterSending__0 to acquiringMeasurements add private synchronous link between Barometer and Controller add connection from BarometerData in Barometer to BarometerData in Controller in private synchronous link add state readingBarometerData in Controller add state afterReceiving__7 in Controller add action on signal receiveBarometerData in Controller with BarometerData(pressure) add transition in Controller from computingDerivedMeasurements to readingBarometerData add transition in Controller from readingBarometerData to receiveBarometerData add transition in Controller from receiveBarometerData to afterReceiving__7 with "pressure = pressure" add transition in Controller from afterReceiving__7 to sendingMeasurements remove transition in Controller from computingDerivedMeasurements to sendingMeasurements
Description: The specification requires a barometer sensor and transmission of barometric pressure, but the model does not contain a Barometer block/part or a connection that provides BarometerData to the Controller. As a result, pressure cannot be acquired from the required sensor path.
How to solve: Add a dedicated Barometer block and part that emits BarometerData, connect it to Controller.BarometerData, and update the Controller behavior so it reads BarometerData before sending measurements to the VHF transmitter.

infoparam.name: UML Note
infoparam.value:
*Well-formed executable* suggestion Computation time: 205 s Semantic oracle evaluation: 4
Our evaluation: Suggestion relevance: 9.5 Mutation script accuracy wrt to the suggestion (how to solve): 9.5 Model improvement: 8.5 Comments: improvement of the model wrt the identified issue. Yet it is still needed to modify two transition's in the Controller's SMD and one in the Barometer's SMD to achieve full semantic accuracy.
Mutations: add block Barometer add attribute int pressure in Barometer add output signal BarometerData(int pressure) in Barometer add state acquiringMeasurements in Barometer add transition in Barometer from start to acquiringMeasurements add connection from BarometerData in Barometer to BarometerData in Controller in private synchronous link add state readingBarometerData in Controller add transition in Controller from readingGNSSData to readingBarometerData add transition in Controller from readingBarometerData to readingAnemometerData
Description: The specification requires the buoy to be equipped with a barometer and to transmit barometric pressure, but the model has no Barometer block, no barometer part instance, and no connection providing Controller.BarometerData even though the controller declares that input.
How to solve: Add a Barometer block with an output signal carrying pressure, instantiate it in the system structure, connect it to Controller.BarometerData, and update the controller state machine so barometer data is actually acquired before transmission.

infoparam.name: UML Note
infoparam.value:
Suggestion. Computation time: 405 s Semantic oracle evaluation (for the SysML v2 specification returned): 10
Our evaluation: Suggestion relevance: 9.5 Model improvement: 0
Description: The specification requires a barometer sensor and transmission of barometric pressure, but the model has no Barometer block/part or connection to the Controller, and the Controller behavior never reads BarometerData before sending pressure to the VHF transmitter. As a result, pressure is modeled as transmitted without a specified acquisition source.
How to solve: Add a Barometer block and corresponding part with an output flow for BarometerData, connect it to Controller.BarometerData, and update the Controller state machine to acquire BarometerData before computing and sending measurements so that pressure originates from the required sensor.

Line.value: Computation time: 405 s
Semantic oracle evaluation (for the SysML v2 specification returned): 10
Our evaluation:
Suggestion relevance: 9.5
Model improvement: 0
The specification requires a barometer sensor and transmission of barometric pressure, but the model has no Barometer block/part or

connection to the Controller, and the Controller behavior never reads BarometerData before sending pressure to the VHF transmitter. As a result, pressure is modeled as transmitted without a specified acquisition source.
How to solve:

Add a Barometer block and corresponding part with an output flow for BarometerData, connect it to Controller.BarometerData, and update the Controller state machine to acquire BarometerData before computing and
sending measurements so that pressure originates from the required sensor.

infoparam.name: UML Note
infoparam.value:
*Well-formed executable* suggestion Computation time: 173 s Semantic oracle evaluation: 4
Our evaluation: Suggestion relevance: 9.5 Mutation script accuracy wrt to the suggestion (how to solve): 9 Model improvement: 9 Comments: improvement of the model wrt the identified issue. Yet it is still needed to modify a transition in the Controller's SMD and in the Barometer's SMD to achieve full semantic accuracy.
Mutations: add block Barometer add attribute int pressure in Barometer add output signal BarometerData(int pressure) in Barometer add state acquiringMeasurements in Barometer add state afterSending__0 in Barometer add transition in Barometer from start to acquiringMeasurements add transition in Barometer from acquiringMeasurements to afterSending__0 add transition in Barometer from afterSending__0 to acquiringMeasurements add private synchronous link between Barometer and Controller add connection from BarometerData in Barometer to BarometerData in Controller in private synchronous link
Description: The specification requires the buoy to be equipped with a barometer and to transmit barometric pressure, but the model does not include any Barometer block, no barometer part instance, and no connection that supplies the Controller.BarometerData input. As a result, the pressure acquisition path required by the specification is incomplete.
How to solve: Add a Barometer block with an output item carrying pressure, instantiate it as a part, connect it to Controller.BarometerData, and include its acquisition behavior consistently with the other sensors so the controller can receive pressure before transmission.

add private synchronous link between Barometer and Controller
add connection from BarometerData in Barometer to BarometerData in Controller in private synchronous link
The specification requires the buoy to be equipped with a barometer and to transmit barometric pressure, but the model does not include any Barometer block, no barometer
part instance, and no connection that supplies the Controller.BarometerData input. As a result, the pressure acquisition path required by the specification is incomplete.
How to solve:
Add a Barometer block with an output item carrying pressure, instantiate it as a part, connect it to Controller.BarometerData, and include its acquisition behavior consistently
with the other sensors so the controller can receive pressure before transmission.

Description: The model is missing the barometer sensor required by the specification. Although the Controller declares an input for BarometerData and transmits pressure, there is no Barometer block, no BarometerData producer, and no connection to the Controller, so barometric pressure cannot be acquired as specified.
How to solve: Add a Barometer block with a BarometerData output carrying pressure, instantiate it, connect it to Controller.BarometerData, and extend the Controller behavior to receive and store that pressure value before transmission.

infoparam.name: UML Note
infoparam.value:
*Well-formed executable* suggestion Computation time: 218 s Semantic oracle evaluation: 8
Our evaluation: Suggestion relevance: 10 Mutation script accuracy wrt to the suggestion (how to solve): 9 Model improvement: 9.5 Comments: model has been improved wrt the identified issue. It is still needed to modify one transition in the Barometer's SMD to achieve full semantic accuracy.
Mutations: add block Barometer add attribute int pressure in Barometer add output signal BarometerData(int pressure) in Barometer add state acquiringMeasurements in Barometer add transition in Barometer from start to acquiringMeasurements add transition in Barometer from acquiringMeasurements to acquiringMeasurements with "BarometerData(pressure)" add private synchronous link between Barometer and Controller add connection from BarometerData in Barometer to BarometerData in Controller in private synchronous link add state readingBarometerData in Controller remove transition in Controller from computingDerivedMeasurements to sendingMeasurements add transition in Controller from computingDerivedMeasurements to readingBarometerData add action on signal receiveBarometerData in Controller with BarometerData(pressure) add transition in Controller from readingBarometerData to receiveBarometerData add transition in Controller from receiveBarometerData to sendingMeasurements
Description: The specification requires the buoy to be equipped with a barometer and to transmit barometric pressure via the VHF transmitter, but the model has no Barometer part, no connection feeding the controller from such a sensor, and no controller behavior that acquires BarometerData before transmission.
How to solve: Add a dedicated Barometer block and instance, define and connect its BarometerData flow to the Controller, and extend the Controller state machine so it explicitly receives BarometerData and updates pressure before sending the transmission payload.

Our evaluation: Suggestion relevance: 9.5 Model improvement: 0
Description: The specification requires a barometer sensor and transmission of barometric pressure, but the model has no instantiated Barometer block, no connection feeding Controller.BarometerData, and the controller behavior never acquires pressure before sending data to the VHF transmitter.
How to solve: Add a Barometer part with an output item carrying pressure, connect it to Controller.BarometerData, and extend the Controller state machine with a pressure-acquisition step that stores the received value before computing derived measurements and transmitting the measurement set.

infoparam.name: UML Note
infoparam.value:
*Suggestion* Computation time: 385 s Semantic oracle assessment of the SysML v2 fallback: 10
Our evaluation: Suggestion relevance: 9.5 Model improvement: 0
Description: The model is missing the barometer sensing chain required by the specification. Although pressure is listed in transmitted data and a controller input type for barometer data exists, there is no Barometer block, no instantiated barometer part, no connection to the Controller, and no controller reception step that updates pressure from barometer measurements.
How to solve: Add a Barometer sensor block and corresponding signal type, instantiate the barometer in the system structure, connect it to the Controller, and extend the Controller state machine with a reception step for barometer data before derived-data computation and VHF transmission.

the specification. Although pressure is listed in transmitted data and a controller input type for barometer data exists, there is no Barometer block, no instantiated barometer part, no connection to the Controller, and no controller reception step that updates pressure from barometer measurements.
How to solve:

Add a Barometer sensor block and corresponding signal type, instantiate the barometer in the system structure, connect it to the Controller, and extend the Controller state machine with a reception step for barometer data before derived-data computation and VHF transmission.

infoparam.name: UML Note
infoparam.value:
*Well-formed executable* suggestion Computation time: 377 s Semantic oracle evaluation: 7
Our evaluation: Suggestion relevance: 10 Mutation script accuracy wrt to the suggestion (how to solve): 9 Model improvement: 9.5 Comments: model improved wrt the identified issue. Yet the Barometer's SMD does not sends the signal, so it is still needed to modify one transition.
Mutations: add block Barometer add attribute int pressure in Barometer add output signal BarometerData(int pressure) in Barometer add state acquiringMeasurements in Barometer add transition in Barometer from start to acquiringMeasurements add transition in Barometer from acquiringMeasurements to acquiringMeasurements with "BarometerData(pressure)" add connection from BarometerData in Barometer to BarometerData in Controller in private synchronous link add state readingBarometerData in Controller add action on signal receiveBarometerData in Controller with BarometerData(pressure) remove transition in Controller from computingDerivedMeasurements to sendingMeasurements add transition in Controller from computingDerivedMeasurements to readingBarometerData add transition in Controller from readingBarometerData to receiveBarometerData add transition in Controller from receiveBarometerData to sendingMeasurements with "pressure = pressure"
Description: The specification requires a barometer sensor whose measurements are acquired by the controller and transmitted as barometric pressure, but the model has no Barometer block, no Barometer part instance, and no connection producing Controller.BarometerData.
How to solve: Add a Barometer block with a state machine that acquires and sends pressure data, instantiate it in the system structure, connect its output to Controller.BarometerData, and extend the controller behavior to receive that input before transmission.

Line.value: *Well-formed executable* suggestion
Computation time: 377 s
Semantic oracle evaluation: 7
Our evaluation:
Suggestion relevance: 10
Mutation script accuracy wrt to the suggestion (how to solve): 9
Model improvement: 9.5
Comments: model improved wrt the identified issue. Yet the Barometer's SMD does not sends the signal, so it is still needed to modify one transition.
add block Barometer
add attribute int pressure in Barometer
add output signal BarometerData(int pressure) in Barometer
add state acquiringMeasurements in Barometer
add transition in Barometer from start to acquiringMeasurements
add transition in Barometer from acquiringMeasurements to acquiringMeasurements with "BarometerData(pressure)"
add connection from BarometerData in Barometer to BarometerData in Controller in private synchronous link
add state readingBarometerData in Controller
add action on signal receiveBarometerData in Controller with BarometerData(pressure)
remove transition in Controller from computingDerivedMeasurements to sendingMeasurements
add transition in Controller from computingDerivedMeasurements to readingBarometerData
add transition in Controller from readingBarometerData to receiveBarometerData
add transition in Controller from receiveBarometerData to sendingMeasurements with "pressure = pressure"
The specification requires a barometer sensor whose measurements are acquired by the controller and transmitted as barometric pressure, but the model has no Barometer block,
no Barometer part instance, and no connection producing Controller.BarometerData.
How to solve:
Add a Barometer block with a state machine that acquires and sends pressure data, instantiate it in the system structure, connect its output to Controller.BarometerData, and
extend the controller behavior to receive that input before transmission.
